vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by visual evidence. P1 (Yellow Pot) is present but appears devoid of foliage. P3 (Black Pot) contains the white rabbit anchor, but the Pothos is not visible. P2 and P4 are indistinguishable or absent.

inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss (Pothos absent; only anchor remains)

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface, no String of Nickels detected.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; soil surface appears barren of Pothos foliage.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears dark and potentially saturated, but lacks organic growth.
biome_observations.debris: White rabbit anchor in P3 is the only non-soil element.

temporal_deltas.EARLIEST_to_T-2: Minimal change; both images show empty pots.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Loss of visibility due to lighting conditions; no growth detected.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: No recovery or new growth observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of total failure. There is no evidence of living plant tissue in any of the pots. The environment is currently a 'dead' system.
vision_report.anomalies: The primary anomaly is the complete absence of the registered flora despite the presence of the pots and the scale anchor.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image sequence. My first step was to establish the baseline against the registry, followed by a pixel-by-pixel scan of the pot interiors. I validate that the biome is currently non-viable. The pots are present, but the biological occupants are entirely missing or have perished, leaving only the white rabbit anchor in P3. No incidental growth or secondary seedlings were found. The system is currently in a state of total botanical collapse.
